name: Lezgian
notes: Lezgian (лезги чӏал) of Dagestan and Azerbaijan, written in a Cyrillic alphabet. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Lezgian has a large consonant inventory with a four-way stop contrast (voiced / voiceless aspirated / voiceless unaspirated / ejective) and uvular and pharyngeal series, encoded by Cyrillic digraphs and the palochka ⟨ӏ⟩; establishing the letter/digraph → IPA table reliably requires a proper descriptive source (Haspelmath's grammar), which has not been consulted. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode lezg1247), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode lezg1247.

ancestors.notes: Nakh-Daghestanian — the classification node this language sits under.
